1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`). Iter-012 opened Path-2 Čech scaffold (`Scheme.cechCochain_OC`, `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`), both honest one-liner closures. Iter-014 closed the Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite `Scheme.HModule'` (cohomology of an open in `ModuleCat k` flavor). Iter-015 closed the algebraic preliminary `Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` (`H⁰` bridge for `HModule'`). Iter-016 closed the functorial wrappers `Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` (substantive bifunctor `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`) and `Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` (per-fiber abbrev `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`), completing the Mayer-Vietoris LES prerequisites on the `ModuleCat k` side. **Iter-017 attacks the LES directly**: the first two LES building blocks `HModule'_toBiprod` (sum of two restriction maps) and `HModule'_fromBiprod` (difference of two restriction maps), mirroring Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` and `fromBiprod` (file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46 and L67–70) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Iter-018+ will add the composition-is-zero lemma `toBiprod_fromBiprod` and the connecting hom `δ`; iter-019+ will add the LES sequence, the iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`, and the exactness theorem. Iter-020+ will specialise to a 2-affine cover of a proper $k$-curve to attack Serre finiteness — the multi-iteration theorem that `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (one of the four `Jacobian.lean` instances) will eventually consume.

In this iteration's first pass (the current one) the plan agent has only written the refactor directive and this PROGRESS.md; the dispatcher will run the refactor agent next, then re-invoke the plan agent for post-refactor verification, then run the prover agent on this objective.

The plan-agent post-prover verification will accept either outcome.

52	## Current Objectives
53	
54	Single-file objective for the iter-017 prover round (post-refactor). The refactor sub-phase scaffolds two new declarations in unprotected territory; the prover sub-phase closes both with probe-confirmed term-mode one-liners.
55	
56	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`** — close the `HModule'_toBiprod` and `HModule'_fromBiprod` bodies.
57	
58	**Blueprint reference:** `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, the section "Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks: $\toBiprod$ and $\fromBiprod$ (iter-017)" containing `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod` and `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod` (already written by the iter-017 plan-agent first pass, this iteration), plus the remarks "Why these are the iter-017 targets" and "Compatibility with Mathlib's $\AddCommGrpCat$-flavored LES" giving the broader Mayer-Vietoris context.
59	
60	#### Goal
61	
62	Close two `:= sorry` bodies with probe-confirmed term-mode one-liners. The new declarations `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod` will be inserted by the iter-017 refactor agent inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, immediately after `Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` (currently L297–L304) and before the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` (currently L306).
63	
64	The signatures (per the iter-017 refactor directive):
65	
66	```lean
67	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
68	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
69	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
70	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
71	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
72	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
73	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
74	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
75	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) := sorry
76	
77	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
78	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
79	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
80	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
81	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
82	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
83	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
84	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
85	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) := sorry
86	```
87	
88	Note carefully:
89	- Both declarations are `noncomputable def` (matches Mathlib L43, L67); not `abbrev`. **Do not swap.**
90	- All three context arguments are explicit (`(S : J.MayerVietorisSquare)`, `(F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))`, `(n : ℕ)`), even though Mathlib uses `variable` to make `S` and `F` parameters of the namespace — we keep them explicit because we are not inside a `MayerVietorisSquare`-namespace block.
91	- Codomain shapes `... ⟶ ... ⊞ ...` and `... ⊞ ... ⟶ ...` use the biproduct in `AddCommGrpCat`. **Do not change to product `×` or coproduct `⊕`.** The biproduct is forced by Mathlib's `biprod.lift` / `biprod.desc` API.
92	- The pairing `(S.f₂₄, S.f₃₄)` for toBiprod and `(S.f₁₂, -S.f₁₃)` for fromBiprod is the Mayer-Vietoris convention (Mathlib L45–46, L69–70); **do not swap and do not drop the negation**.
93	
94	#### Probe-confirmed closure bodies
95	
96	Replace the `sorry` bodies with the term-mode one-liners below. **Both bodies have been probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`) by the iter-017 plan-agent against the post-iter-016 file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`** under the file's actual `open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry` directive — drop-in, no adjustment expected. The refactor agent will additionally re-run the same probe against the post-refactor file as a sanity check (per directive §5).
97	
98	```lean
99	-- Body for HModule'_toBiprod (replaces `sorry`)
100	biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
101	            ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
102	
103	-- Body for HModule'_fromBiprod (replaces `sorry`)
104	biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
105	            (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
106	```
107	
108	Notes on name resolution (relevant if Lean elaboration surfaces a unification ambiguity that the live probes missed):
109	
110	- The file has `open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry` at L38. Inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, `biprod.lift`, `biprod.desc`, and the infix biproduct `⊞` all auto-resolve via `open Limits` (they live in `CategoryTheory.Limits.biprod`). The unqualified `biprod.lift` / `biprod.desc` work; equivalent fully-qualified spellings: `CategoryTheory.Limits.biprod.lift` / `CategoryTheory.Limits.biprod.desc`. Both work.
111	- The body uses the **in-namespace short name** `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` (no `Scheme.` prefix). Inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, writing `Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` would resolve to `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` (double prefix) and fail elaboration. Equivalent fully-qualified spelling for the body if needed: `(AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op`.
112	- The unary minus on the morphism `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` resolves via the preadditive structure of `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms (auto-inferable). Equivalent explicit form: `Neg.neg ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)`.
113	- Do **not** rename the declarations, change the codomain shape, change the namespace, swap `def` and `abbrev`, swap the pairing of `f` indices, drop the negation, or alter any prior declaration in the file.
114	
115	#### Verification the prover should run before reporting
116	
117	1. `lean_diagnostic_messages /home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should return `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (no warnings, no errors).
118	2. `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod` should return `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}`.
119	3. `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod` should return `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}`.
120	4. `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` should return `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`; the file `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should not appear in the list).
121	
122	#### Forbidden shortcuts (iter-017 specific)

22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
